jokes.text: Law & Order parody opening with 'goofballs' and 'eggheads'
jokes.setup: Community opens with mock Law & Order intro
jokes.punchline: Describes community college as crime procedural with 'goofballs' and 'eggheads'
jokes.explanation: Perfect genre parody opening that sets up the entire episode's conceit. Clever recontextualization of Law & Order format for community college setting.

jokes.text: Hey, she's the one putting her hands in my mouth
jokes.setup: Jeff explains why he keeps hitting on dental hygienist
jokes.punchline: Innocent dental procedure framed as sexual advance
jokes.explanation: Decent setup but relies on obvious innuendo. Character-appropriate for Jeff's sleazy lawyer persona.

jokes.text: That's the first time those words have actually been true
jokes.setup: Character says 'nothing to see here'
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges for once there literally is nothing interesting
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the cliché phrase. Clean structure with nice economy.

jokes.text: Why don't I just get pregnant at a bus station?
jokes.setup: Annie upset about getting a C grade
jokes.punchline: Wildly inappropriate analogy comparing C grade to teen pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie overreaction. Completely absurd escalation that's both shocking and character-specific. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Now if you'll excuse me, I was watching Mama's Family
jokes.setup: Professor dismisses murder investigation
jokes.punchline: Returns to watching lowbrow sitcom amid CSI parody
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kane's disinterest. Specific pop culture reference adds authenticity.

jokes.text: You let me handle the nitty gritty
jokes.setup: Annie takes charge of investigation
jokes.punchline: Uses cop show cliché language inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Annie channeling TV cops. Functional but predictable character beat.

jokes.text: It's been a long 15 years. Looks like it's gonna be a late night
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed adopt cop show personas
jokes.punchline: Treating college yam case like hardboiled detective work
jokes.explanation: Great parody of cop show dialogue. Perfect for Troy and Abed's genre-awareness.

jokes.text: It's not a short straw. It's a hot potato. Yeah, well, looks pretty cold to me
jokes.setup: Mixing metaphors about their assignment
jokes.punchline: Abed literalizes 'cold' vs hot potato
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay. Abed's literal interpretation of metaphor is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Cold? Or dead?
jokes.setup: Following up on 'cold' reference
jokes.punchline: Dramatically escalates to murder terminology
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that maintains cop show parody. Abed's dramatic delivery implied.

jokes.text: We can't both do the zinger
jokes.setup: Both try to deliver dramatic one-liner
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on cop show tropes
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta moment. Troy breaking character to address genre convention is very Community.

jokes.text: Okay, okay, five-to-one on the black guy
jokes.setup: Pierce making bets on investigation
jokes.punchline: Casual racism disguised as gambling odds
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce casual racism. Character-appropriate but relies on offensive stereotype for humor.

jokes.text: That's offensive. Fine, six-to-one
jokes.setup: Called out for offensive comment
jokes.punchline: Makes it worse by increasing the odds instead of apologizing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Pierce moment. Completely misses the point and makes it worse, perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Youth! Scatter!
jokes.setup: Pierce approaches group of young people
jokes.punchline: Dramatically announces himself like a villain
jokes.explanation: Great Pierce line. Treats his presence as inherently threatening to youth. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I... fell asleep in a sunbeam
jokes.setup: Pierce explains why he was late
jokes.punchline: Admits to cat-like behavior as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Pierce moment. Humanizes him while being completely ridiculous.

jokes.text: Actually it is. I used to live with him, it's kind of adorable
jokes.setup: Someone doubts Pierce's sunbeam story
jokes.punchline: Troy confirms it's real and calls elderly Pierce 'adorable'
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback that validates Pierce's absurd behavior while showing Troy's affection.

jokes.text: Duh-doy! Todd
jokes.setup: Asked who would want to hurt them
jokes.punchline: Says obvious like it's profound with made-up word
jokes.explanation: Pierce's weird made-up exclamation. Character-specific speech pattern.

jokes.text: Cut yourself on an extra sharp Oscar Wilde play?
jokes.setup: Todd has a bandage, Troy investigates
jokes.punchline: Suggests injury from 'sharp' wit of Oscar Wilde
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'sharp wit.' Surprisingly sophisticated for Troy but works as cop parody.

jokes.text: Why not raisin?
jokes.setup: Todd explains it was a blueberry muffin assignment
jokes.punchline: Abed asks random follow-up question about muffin type
jokes.explanation: Typical Abed interrogation technique. Seemingly random but purposeful detective work.

jokes.text: You can dance all day, Todd, but sooner or later, the music's gonna change
jokes.setup: Troy trying to be intimidating detective
jokes.punchline: Uses overwrought metaphor that sounds profound but means nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of detective intimidation tactics. Troy channeling hard-boiled dialogue badly.

jokes.text: old west color. I don't know. I thought it was cool
jokes.setup: Abed enhances photo for investigation
jokes.punchline: Just makes it sepia tone for no investigative reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment. Gets distracted by aesthetics during serious investigation. Very in character.

jokes.text: Eight plus two times five... Ten after 8:00. I'm gifted in other ways
jokes.setup: Britta tries to do simple math
jokes.punchline: Gets it wrong, defensively claims other talents
jokes.explanation: Classic Britta incompetence. Math error followed by defensive self-preservation.

jokes.text: Magnitude.
jokes.setup: Building suspense about who checked out the key
jokes.punchline: It's Magnitude, the one-note character
jokes.explanation: Good reveal that uses established character. The suspense buildup makes the mundane revelation funny.

jokes.text: Pop pop!
jokes.setup: Magnitude is questioned about key theft
jokes.punchline: Responds with his only catchphrase instead of useful information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Magnitude moment. Can only say his catchphrase even during serious interrogation.

jokes.text: That doesn't make sense. You don't order ketchup. It's a condiment!
jokes.setup: Abed says 'order some ketchup' as tough cop
jokes.punchline: Troy breaks character to point out logical flaw
jokes.explanation: Brilliant moment where Troy's logic overrides his roleplay. Very character-specific response.

jokes.text: Troy, Troy, hey, walk it off! Troy, walk it off!
jokes.setup: Troy gets agitated during interrogation
jokes.punchline: Abed treats him like an athlete having a meltdown
jokes.explanation: Good buddy cop dynamic. Abed managing Troy's emotional outburst like a sports coach.

jokes.text: Sorry, my friend, he's a bad cop. Yeah. I'm a good cop. Oh, good. You can trust me
jokes.setup: Abed explains good cop/bad cop routine
jokes.punchline: Star-Burns naively accepts the obvious manipulation
jokes.explanation: Standard good cop/bad cop parody. Star-Burns being gullible adds to the joke.

jokes.text: And you got nothing on me. And I don't have to stay here, 'cause you're not cops
jokes.setup: Star-Burns being interrogated by fake cops
jokes.punchline: Points out obvious flaw in their authority
jokes.explanation: Logical reality check that deflates the cop roleplay. Star-Burns being occasionally smart.

jokes.text: He's right. You're not cops. I'd say our hands are tied, but we basically have no hands
jokes.setup: Jeff acknowledges their lack of authority
jokes.punchline: Escalates metaphor to complete powerlessness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the metaphor. Jeff's lawyerly way of admitting defeat.

jokes.text: I can both see and hear you
jokes.setup: Group discusses strategy while Star-Burns is present
jokes.punchline: Points out they're having 'secret' conversation in front of him
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to classic TV trope of discussing plans in front of suspect.

jokes.text: Sure, if the gardener's Joseph Mengele
jokes.setup: Asked if yam damage could be natural
jokes.punchline: References Nazi doctor to describe plant abuse
jokes.explanation: Dark historical reference for plant forensics. Appropriately over-the-top for CSI parody.

jokes.text: You need a boot
jokes.setup: Explains what it takes to damage yam that badly
jokes.punchline: Delivers dramatic conclusion about footwear
jokes.explanation: Good CSI parody moment. Dramatic delivery of mundane conclusion.

jokes.text: Well, whoever did do this does owe you guys a letter. An 'A.'
jokes.setup: Responds to Jeff's love letter comment
jokes.punchline: Turns it into pun about getting A grade
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay callback to Jeff's sarcasm. Shirley being supportive while clever.

jokes.text: We named the yam 'Pam.' It rhymed
jokes.setup: Reveals they named their yam
jokes.punchline: Explains obvious rhyme like it's profound
jokes.explanation: Sweet Annie moment. Explaining obvious rhyme shows her earnest nature.

jokes.text: Keep the change, Garret. You know what? Keep the hot dog
jokes.setup: Star-Burns being generous to Garrett
jokes.punchline: Escalates generosity to giving away his lunch
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation joke. Star-Burns being unexpectedly magnanimous.

jokes.text: And you're sure you heard a kitten meowing in here? Not anymore, so hurry up
jokes.setup: Troy questions their breaking-in excuse
jokes.punchline: Abed implies kitten is now dead, making excuse worse
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy. Abed making their fake excuse accidentally more disturbing.

jokes.text: Whoa, Nelly!
jokes.setup: Abed discovers Star-Burns' locker contents
jokes.punchline: Uses old-fashioned exclamation for drug discovery
jokes.explanation: Abed using antiquated expression. Contrast between discovery and reaction.

jokes.text: Clean up on aisle 'busted'
jokes.setup: Discovering Star-Burns' illegal activity
jokes.punchline: Mixes store announcement with arrest terminology
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay. Troy mixing retail and police terminology cleverly.

jokes.text: Why do they always r... Why do they always... You go ahead. Why do they always run?
jokes.setup: Star-Burns flees, Troy starts cop show cliché
jokes.punchline: Gets tangled up saying the line, Abed helps finish it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy and Abed moment. Meta-commentary on cop show tropes with character-specific execution.

jokes.text: Kiss me! What? I'll explain later. No. I'll explain later. The explanation isn't the issue
jokes.setup: Star-Burns caught, needs distraction
jokes.punchline: Tries to kiss Jeff as escape tactic, Jeff focuses on wrong problem
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment. Jeff's lawyer brain focusing on wrong aspect of situation.

jokes.text: Look, I-I swiped some beakers for the meth lab that I'm building in the trunk of my car
jokes.setup: Star-Burns explains his illegal activity
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to building meth lab like it's hobby
jokes.explanation: Classic Star-Burns criminality. Treats meth lab like mundane project.

jokes.text: Pinky swear?
jokes.setup: Star-Burns negotiating immunity deal
jokes.punchline: Wants childish oath for serious criminal matter
jokes.explanation: Great contrast. Serious criminal wanting kindergarten promise. Very Star-Burns.

jokes.text: He weaves baskets and lies
jokes.setup: Confronting Todd about his hobby and deception
jokes.punchline: Parallel structure treating basket weaving and lying equally
jokes.explanation: Excellent parallel structure. Abed's precise, almost poetic accusation style.

jokes.text: Todd Jacobson, you have the right to do whatever you want. Nothing you say or do can be used against you by anyone, but we'd really like it if you came with us. Please and thank you
jokes.setup: Troy attempts to read Miranda rights
jokes.punchline: Gets everything backwards, makes it polite request
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Troy moment. Perfect inversion of Miranda rights into polite request. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: A pinky swear? This is ludicrous. This whole school is ludicrous
jokes.setup: Jeff mentions pinky swear as legal obligation
jokes.punchline: Military officer's disgust at childish legal system
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier absurd moment. Colonel's outrage at community college justice system.

jokes.text: Yo, man, you spent your life in the army. I spent mine in prison. Two places where a man's word is only as good as his uniform
jokes.setup: Kane compares military and prison experience
jokes.punchline: Equates army and prison as places where appearance equals credibility
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Professor Kane moment. Profound observation about institutional authority delivered with street wisdom. Highly quotable and character-defining.

jokes.text: Now out here, it's supposed to be different. And in my class, it will be
jokes.setup: Kane's philosophy about outside institutions vs. education
jokes.punchline: Takes moral stand for educational integrity over yam case
jokes.explanation: Powerful character moment. Kane's idealism about education creating moral standard. Great follow-up to previous line.

jokes.text: A man's gotta have a code
jokes.setup: Kane explains his principled stance
jokes.punchline: References The Wire/Omar Little's famous philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect TV reference for Kane's character. Omar's code applied to community college biology.

jokes.text: Abed! Abed! Abed! Walk it off! Walk it off!
jokes.setup: Abed gets agitated like Troy did earlier
jokes.punchline: Troy uses same calming technique Abed used on him
jokes.explanation: Nice role reversal callback. Troy caring for Abed using Abed's own technique.

jokes.text: I'm sorry about my partner. He... he's been on edge ever since we switched
jokes.setup: Troy apologizes for Abed's outburst
jokes.punchline: References their role swap from earlier scene
jokes.explanation: Good callback to their dynamic shift. Troy showing emotional intelligence about their partnership.

jokes.text: A man who for all we know is a holocaust-denying, 9/11 pedophile
jokes.setup: Colonel attacking Star-Burns' credibility
jokes.punchline: Randomly combines worst possible accusations
jokes.explanation: Over-the-top character assassination. Slightly cheap shock value but fits military lawyer aggression.

jokes.text: A hero doesn't call himself a hero. A hero gets called a hero because of what he does
jokes.setup: Annie questions Todd's heroism
jokes.punchline: Builds him up before tearing him down
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Annie's cross-examination. Classic prosecution technique of false praise.

jokes.text: And I gotta tell you, Todd. Couldn't find a thing. Not one little smudge. Except, of course, for one pesky yam that just wouldn't grow
jokes.setup: Annie claims Todd has perfect record
jokes.punchline: Reveals the one flaw that drives the case
jokes.explanation: Excellent courtroom drama parody. Annie's prosecutorial buildup to the key weakness.

jokes.text: Is that why you hit your wife? Withdrawn. Is that why you drink and pop pills? Withdrawn. Are you a virgin? Withdrawn
jokes.setup: Annie's aggressive cross-examination
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire irrelevant personal attacks all withdrawn
jokes.explanation: Brilliant courtroom parody. Annie going full prosecutor with increasingly personal attacks, all immediately withdrawn.

jokes.text: No, you zip it, Uncle Knobby
jokes.setup: Colonel tells Todd to be quiet
jokes.punchline: Todd snaps with ridiculous nickname for military officer
jokes.explanation: Great character break. Todd's rebellion using absurd nickname 'Uncle Knobby' for intimidating colonel.

jokes.text: I'm not fighting your wars anymore
jokes.setup: Todd continuing his rebellion against Colonel
jokes.punchline: Treats yam investigation like military campaign
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Todd's breakdown. Military metaphor for school drama.

jokes.text: Defuse the I.E.D. of dishonesty
jokes.setup: Annie pushing Todd to tell truth
jokes.punchline: Uses military metaphor for confession
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor mixing military and emotional language. Annie adapting to Todd's background.

jokes.text: Booyah! That's how it's done! ♪ Ooh, ooh, ooh, ah, ah, ah, ah ♪
jokes.setup: Annie wins confession
jokes.punchline: Excessive victory celebration with tribal dancing
jokes.explanation: Annie's competitive side showing through. Victory dance is character-appropriate excess.

jokes.text: I'm telling your mother
jokes.setup: Colonel threatens Todd for confession
jokes.punchline: Military officer's ultimate threat is telling mommy
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of military authority. Ultimate punishment is parental disappointment.

jokes.text: Always have an exit strategy
jokes.setup: Colonel leaves after threatening Todd
jokes.punchline: Applies military wisdom to awkward social situation
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency. Military advice for embarrassing retreat.

jokes.text: A man's got to have a code. I can only assume there is a female equivalent to that. A 'codette' or something
jokes.setup: Jeff references Kane's earlier philosophy
jokes.punchline: Makes up ridiculous feminine version of 'code'
jokes.explanation: Jeff making up words while trying to be philosophical. 'Codette' is appropriately ridiculous.

jokes.text: It's just a stupid yam in a stupid jar
jokes.setup: Annie beginning to question their investigation
jokes.punchline: Reduces entire case to its absurd core
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie moment of clarity. Cuts through all the drama to basic truth.

jokes.text: We're all fully-grown adults failing a kindergarten project.
jokes.setup: Questioning why yam growing was so difficult
jokes.punchline: Harsh but accurate assessment of their academic abilities
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware moment about Greendale's academic standards. Annie's harsh but accurate self-assessment.

jokes.text: Boiling water is the icicle stabbing of yam killing
jokes.setup: Annie explains murder weapon
jokes.punchline: Creates absurd metaphor mixing temperature extremes
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor. Annie's prosecutorial mind creating vivid but nonsensical comparison.

jokes.text: This yam isn't boiled. Vicki?
jokes.setup: Annie's theory seems to work
jokes.punchline: Evidence doesn't match, accidentally calls out wrong person
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection. Annie's confidence undermined by evidence, wrongly accuses innocent.

jokes.text: I boiled the yams. Vicki's yam never sprouted and I didn't want her to fail, so I threw off the grading curve so she wouldn't have to go to summer school and we can finally have sex in my parents' cabin
jokes.setup: Neil confesses to yam murders
jokes.punchline: Romantic confession escalates to TMI about parents' cabin
jokes.explanation: Perfect Neil moment. Academic crime motivated by pathetic romantic hopes with TMI overshare.

jokes.text: God forgive me, I did it for love!
jokes.setup: Neil's confession about sabotaging the yams
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration about mundane academic crime
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic conclusion to Neil's confession. Treats academic sabotage with soap opera gravity.

jokes.text: God forgive me, I did it for love!
jokes.setup: Neil's confession continues
jokes.punchline: Treats yam murder like crime of passion
jokes.explanation: Good melodrama. Neil treating plant destruction like romantic tragedy.

jokes.text: We are definitely dissecting pine cones next year
jokes.setup: Kane witnesses elaborate yam murder case
jokes.punchline: Decides to switch to simpler biology project
jokes.explanation: Good teacher reaction. Kane's practical response to academic chaos.

jokes.text: Star-Burns got rear-ended and the meth lab in his trunk exploded. He's dead
jokes.setup: Jeff gets phone call about Star-Burns
jokes.punchline: Casual delivery of shocking death by meth lab explosion
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation. Callback to earlier meth lab joke becomes tragic reality.

jokes.text: Star-Burns got rear-ended and the meth lab in his trunk exploded. He's dead.
jokes.setup: Phone call with news about Star-Burns
jokes.punchline: Casual delivery of shocking death by meth lab explosion
jokes.explanation: Shocking twist that pays off earlier meth lab joke. Dark humor delivered with perfect casualness by the Dean.

jokes.text: Why don't we have bunk bed conversations?
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed in bunk beds
jokes.punchline: Questions why they don't follow sitcom trope
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on sitcom conventions. Troy's awareness of missing trope.

jokes.text: I think it comes from the word 'bunker'
jokes.setup: Troy asks about etymology of 'bunk bed'
jokes.punchline: Abed gives matter-of-fact wrong answer
jokes.explanation: Typical Abed response. Confident but incorrect etymology delivered deadpan.

jokes.text: Oh, it was done about a week ago. Then why are you still here?
jokes.setup: Troy asks when Abed's apartment will be ready
jokes.punchline: Reveals Abed has been staying longer than necessary
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment revealing Abed enjoys their friendship. Slight awkwardness adds charm.

jokes.text: ♪ Troy and Abed, off to Dreamland ♪ ♪ catching the train to Sleepytown ♪ ♪ and when they wake up ♪ ♪ the Dean'll be here ♪ ♪ staring at you ♪
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed sing bedtime song
jokes.punchline: Song takes creepy turn with Dean watching them sleep
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending song that turns innocent to creepy. Classic Troy and Abed musical moment.

jokes.text: Sweet deans. That's nice... I guess
jokes.setup: Shirley mentions Dean will be watching
jokes.punchline: Troy makes pun on 'sweet dreams' then second-guesses it
jokes.explanation: Good pun followed by Troy's uncertainty about its appropriateness. Self-aware ending.
